title: Sanborn Fire Insurance Map from Texarkana, Bowie County, Texas, October 1885 stitching prep manifest
origin_repository: Library of Congress Geography and Map Division

claim_boundary.verified_fact: This manifest records only planning relationships grounded in the committed sheet review notes and validated LOC sheet derivatives.
claim_boundary.source_based_inference: Anchor selection, candidate neighbors, and future alignment order remain source-based stitching inferences until control points and later reconstruction work are reviewed.
claim_boundary.fictional_gameplay: This manifest creates no fictional gameplay records.

runtime_notes: This file plans later stitching work without creating any stitched image output.
Candidate links are evidence-backed planning links, not proof of exact geometry.
Control points, georeferencing, location extraction, and claim generation remain deferred.

blocking_tasks: Translate numbered index blocks into stitched sheet coverage.
Reconcile rail corridor geometry with adjacent sheets before map alignment.
sheet_plans.notes: Use the index, legend, and scale bar as planning aids only; do not treat this sheet as the direct stitched town base.

blocking_tasks: Normalize cross-sheet references before any stitched-map work.
Separate industrial rail frontage from nearby residential blocks.
sheet_plans.notes: Sheet 2 has an explicit cross-sheet note toward sheet 3 and should be aligned only after the downtown anchor is established.

blocking_tasks: Align the downtown frontage geometry with adjacent sheets before map reconstruction.
Review blocks 71 through 77 before promoting visible businesses into location records.
sheet_plans.notes: Sheet 3 is the densest downtown commercial corridor and is the best current anchor candidate for later stitching.

blocking_tasks: Normalize the diagonal State Line frontage before geometry extraction.
Separate burned-site annotations from active businesses in a later pass.
sheet_plans.notes: Sheet 4 appears to continue the downtown frontage and should be aligned against sheet 3 after State Line geometry is normalized.

blocking_tasks: Trace parcel boundaries through the compress and mill complex before georeferencing.
Separate rail passenger, hotel, and industrial labels into later extraction passes.
sheet_plans.notes: Sheet 5 likely connects to the downtown and Front Street corridor through sheet 3 but remains an industrial rail district until traced more carefully.

links.evidence_basis: Sheet 2 review notes state that cross-sheet direction notes point toward sheet No. 3.
links.notes: Use sheet 3 as the receiving anchor when later control points are collected.

links.evidence_basis: Sheet 3 review notes explicitly connect this sheet to No. 4.
links.notes: Treat the shared downtown frontage as a planning relationship only until geometry is reviewed.

links.evidence_basis: Sheet 3 review notes explicitly connect this sheet to No. 5.
links.notes: Downtown-to-rail-corridor alignment remains deferred until the industrial tract is traced more carefully.
